Scenario.title: Water and Wastewater Infrastructure Targeting via Spear-Phishing and Drive-by Compromise
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple Initial Access is gained through targeted spear-phishing and drive-by compromises, leading to execution of malicious payloads and ultimately impacting water/wastewater operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Employees, Internet-facing assets, Shared DB or directory, Vulnerability
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email, Web, Phishing, Vulnerability exploit

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2015-2360 [CWE-22]: Microsoft Office memory corruption vulnerability
stage.description: The threat actor sends targeted phishing emails to employees with relevant access to water/wastewater systems, exploiting a vulnerability in Microsoft Office to deliver a malicious attachment.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2016-8735 [CWE-200]: Adobe Flash Player use-after-free vulnerability
stage.description: The threat actor exploits a vulnerability in Adobe Flash Player on compromised legitimate websites frequently visited by target employees, leading to drive-by downloads of malware.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-21338 [CWE-94]: Windows PowerShell remoting service unauthenticated access vulnerability
stage.description: Upon successful initial access, the threat actor uses PowerShell to download and execute additional payloads, taking advantage of a vulnerability in the PowerShell remoting service.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2020-17496 [CWE-918]: Apache HTTP Server request smuggling vulnerability
stage.description: The threat actor exploits a vulnerability in the Apache HTTP Server to remotely copy web shells or backdoors onto the system, enabling persistent access.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: After gaining persistence, the threat actor encrypts critical data related to water/wastewater operations using ransomware, demanding payment in exchange for the decryption key.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The threat actor manipulates or disrupts SCADA systems controlling water/wastewater infrastructure, causing operational disruptions and potential safety hazards.

Scenario.summary: This scenario outlines a simple yet effective attack against water and wastewater systems, leveraging targeted spear-phishing, drive-by compromises, and exploitation of vulnerabilities to gain initial access, execute malicious payloads, and ultimately impact critical operations.
